Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new unified sparse framework for large-scale material point method (MPM) simulations has been developed to improve computational efficiency. This framework allows for significant reductions in computational time and memory usage in applications such as geophysical mass flows and visual computing, particularly when the material occupies a small fraction of the computational domain.

 - A unified sparse background-grid framework for MPM simulation has been introduced.
 - The framework increases efficiency by reducing computational time and memory usage by one to two orders of magnitude in sparse cases.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28525

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent developments in artificial intelligence focus on enhancing long-horizon memory question answering, using a new memory interface called Structured Spatiotemporal Scene-Event Memory (S3Mem). This technology improves the efficiency of selecting and retrieving relevant evidence for answering complex queries by organizing historical data into structured units.

 - S3Mem enables efficient retrieval of evidence for long-horizon question answering through a structured memory interface.
 - It significantly reduces the number of evidence tokens needed while maintaining competitive performance on various benchmarks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28831

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in interactive question answering technologies are being made through the introduction of Structured Spatiotemporal Scene-Event Memory (S3Mem). This method enhances long-horizon memory question answering by efficiently selecting relevant evidence from diverse historical data, demonstrating competitive performance in various benchmark tests.

 - S3Mem introduces a novel query-time memory interface for interactive question answering.
 - The method shows improved efficiency in evidence selection, utilizing significantly fewer tokens while maintaining competitive accuracy.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28831

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent research indicates that reinforcement learning (RL) is more effective than supervised fine-tuning (SFT) in retaining the capabilities of large language models, suggesting that RL preserves internal computational circuits better. This study introduces a measure of circuit vulnerability, demonstrating that while SFT adapts faster to new tasks, it significantly disrupts prior capabilities compared to RL.

 - Reinforcement learning retains capabilities of large language models better than supervised fine-tuning.
 - A new measure of circuit vulnerability shows the trade-off between rapid adaptation and capability preservation.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28860

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in machine learning focus on the issue of catastrophic forgetting when fine-tuning large language models, with studies indicating that reinforcement learning (RL) preserves prior capabilities better than supervised fine-tuning (SFT). Researchers have introduced a measure of internal computational circuit vulnerability to compare the effects of RL and SFT, finding a trade-off between rapid adaptation to new tasks and the preservation of existing capabilities. These findings have implications for the development and application of artificial intelligence systems.

 - Reinforcement learning is found to be more effective than supervised fine-tuning in retaining prior capabilities in large language models.
 - A new measure of circuit vulnerability has been introduced to analyze the degradation of internal computational processes under different fine-tuning methods.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28860

Category: Cybersecurity
Importance: 9
AI-driven data centers and energy storage systems are increasingly pivotal in power system operations due to their reliance on real-time data. However, vulnerabilities exist as attackers can exploit data structures to conduct stealthy false data injection attacks. New detection methods are being developed to address these risks by leveraging structural network constraints, improving the ability to differentiate between normal and attacked measurements.

 - The reliance on AI and real-time data in power systems is growing.
 - False data injection attacks pose significant risks to power system operations.
 - New detection techniques are being introduced to enhance the security of power systems.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28912

---


Category: Cybersecurity
Importance: 7
Recent research highlights vulnerabilities in power systems, particularly regarding automated decision-making and data-driven attacks, such as false data injection attacks that can evade detection. A new detection method that leverages network topology has been proposed to improve attack detection and mitigate risks in real-time measurements.

 - Increased reliance on automated decision-making in power systems creates vulnerabilities to sophisticated data-driven attacks.
 - The proposed Cycle-Space Detector improves detection of false data injection attacks by using network topology.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28912

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new multifidelity formulation has been introduced to significantly reduce the computational costs associated with proper orthogonal decomposition (POD) for high-fidelity models. This approach optimally utilizes data from less expensive, lower-fidelity models, achieving comparable accuracy to traditional POD while substantially lowering the computational demands involved in snapshot generation.

 - The multifidelity POD (MFPOD) formulation reallocates computational resources to more extensively sample lower-fidelity models.
 - MFPOD guarantees an unbiased estimate of projection errors and demonstrates robustness against variations in training data.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29213

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new mathematical formulation called multifidelity proper orthogonal decomposition (MFPOD) has been developed to significantly reduce computational costs when modeling high-fidelity systems. By utilizing data from lower-fidelity models, MFPOD enhances accuracy while minimizing expenses associated with snapshot generation, particularly in high-dimensional parameter spaces.

 - MFPOD reduces computational costs by leveraging lower-fidelity models.
 - It maintains accuracy in modeling high-fidelity systems with less training data.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29213

A new web-based interactive assistant called MOOSE-Copilot aims to improve scientific hypothesis discovery by integrating human guidance within the research process. The system allows researchers to interactively steer hypothesis generation, overcoming limitations of autonomous models. This innovation is positioned to make hypothesis discovery more accessible to interdisciplinary researchers.

 - MOOSE-Copilot bridges the gap between exploratory search and fine-grained hypothesis refinement.
 - The system utilizes structured human-AI interactions to improve research outcomes.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29475

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new web-based interactive assistant named MOOSE-Copilot has been developed to assist scientists in hypothesis discovery by addressing the limitations of existing large language models. The system incorporates human-AI interaction protocols to enhance exploratory and fine-grained refinement processes, making the hypothesis discovery workflow accessible to interdisciplinary researchers without requiring command line expertise.

 - MOOSE-Copilot bridges the gap between divergent exploratory searches and convergent refinements in scientific hypothesis discovery.
 - It enables scientists to guide the hypothesis generation process through structured human-AI interactions.

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new watermarking framework called LoRA-Key has been developed to protect copyrights in the context of text-to-image diffusion models. This system offers a user-centric approach, allowing for lightweight and effective ownership verification of Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) modules, facilitating easier redistribution and reuse of these creative assets.

 - LoRA-Key provides a mechanism for watermarking that secures user ownership of LoRA models.
 - The method enables copyright protection without the need for retraining specific models, enhancing usability in open communities.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29569

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new watermarking framework called LoRA-Key has been developed for Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) in text-to-image diffusion models, focusing on user-centric copyright protection. This framework allows for the attachment of a recoverable watermark to various LoRAs without requiring retraining, enhancing ownership verification while maintaining image generation quality.

 - LoRA-Key provides a mechanism for lightweight copyright protection in text-to-image models.
 - The framework supports seamless integration of watermarks across different models without the need for retraining.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29569

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in artificial intelligence focus on improving generalization in deep networks through the introduction of polynomial representations, which serve as a low-dimensional surrogate for neural functions. These representations provide a practical metric for simplicity that is predictive of generalization and can enhance performance across various tasks, including image and text classification and reinforcement learning.

 - Polynomial representations serve as a low-dimensional surrogate for neural functions.
 - A new simplicity metric improves generalization in artificial intelligence tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29823

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in artificial intelligence research include the development of polynomial representations as a method to measure and optimize simplicity in deep learning models. These new representations provide a low-dimensional surrogate for neural functions, enabling improved generalization in various tasks including image and text classification, as well as reinforcement learning.

 - Polynomial representations serve as a new metric for measuring simplicity in neural networks.
 - This approach improves generalization performance across multiple AI tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29823

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in generative modeling have led to the introduction of Midpoint Generative Models, which are based on symmetry in flow matching and propose a new metric called the Midpoint Divergence. This new framework shows competitive performance against existing methods and paves the way for a more effective approach to one-step generative modeling.

 - Midpoint Generative Models improve one-step generative model training.
 - The new Midpoint Divergence metric enhances performance comparison between distributions.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29920

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new framework called Midpoint Generative Models (MGM) has been introduced for training one-step generative models, based on Flow Matching principles. The MGM provides a theoretical approach to generative modeling with potential competitive advantages over existing methods.

 - The Midpoint Generative Models framework utilizes symmetry and interpolation techniques for improved generative modeling.
 - MGM achieves competitive performance compared to other one-step generative modeling methods.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29920

Category: Cybersecurity
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in privacy-preserving data aggregation methods in federated learning are emerging, utilizing homomorphic encryption to enhance security against potential data leakage during the learning process. A new protocol has been proposed that allows for efficient communication over wireless channels while maintaining client-level security, addressing vulnerabilities of existing single-key encryption methods.

 - A new four-phase protocol for privacy-enhanced federated learning using xMK-CKKS has been developed.
 - The protocol ensures secure data aggregation without channel estimation, enhancing communication efficiency.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30123

---


Category: Cybersecurity
Importance: 7
A new protocol has been developed to enhance the privacy of federated learning through a method called xMK-CKKS, which allows aggregation of encrypted data without requiring channel estimation. This protocol is designed to operate over wireless channels and is secure against clients that might attempt to uncover sensitive local updates from other participants.

 - A four-phase protocol improves privacy in federated learning using multi-key homomorphic encryption.
 - The protocol increases security against honest-but-curious clients and operates without channel estimation.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30123

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
AI weather models show promising results for short to medium-range forecasts but struggle with stability in long-term predictions. Issues such as blow-up, drift, and loss of seasonality affect their reliability when predicting beyond two weeks. Research categorizes these failures and examines architectural designs that could stabilize the models for improved performance.

 - AI weather models excel in short-term forecasting but face challenges in long-term predictions.
 - The study identifies three main types of failures in AI weather models: blow-up, drift, and loss of seasonality.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30184

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
AI weather models show promise in short to medium-range forecasts, up to 15 days, but struggle with stability and accuracy in longer predictions. A quantitative analysis categorizes specific types of failures in these models and suggests that model stability depends on handling small spatio-temporal scales effectively.

 - AI weather models achieve good accuracy for forecasts up to 15 days.
 - Longer predictions over one year present challenges in model stability and accuracy.
 - The study categorizes model failures into distinct types, impacting their predictive capabilities.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30184

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in reinforcement learning, particularly in the development of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, have focused on improving real-world dexterous robotic manipulation. A new framework named BORA has been proposed, which combines offline and online learning to enhance the performance of VLA models in executing complex tasks while addressing challenges related to physical execution errors and inefficiencies.

 - BORA is a framework designed to bridge the gap between offline reinforcement learning and online adaptation for robotic manipulation.
 - Extensive evaluations show BORA achieves significant improvements in success rates compared to traditional methods.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30226

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
BORA is a newly proposed framework for improving robotic dexterous manipulation through a combination of offline reinforcement learning and online adaptation. The system aims to enhance the performance of Vision-Language-Action models by addressing challenges associated with high-dimensional hand control and execution errors, demonstrating significant improvements in success rates across various tasks.

 - BORA integrates offline and online learning to optimize robotic performance.
 - The framework achieves a 33% increase in success rates for dexterous tasks compared to traditional methods.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30226

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Rapid advancements in autonomous systems are being explored in the context of large language models (LLMs) capable of self-driven data engineering for specialized model training. Recent studies indicate that LLMs like GPT-5.2 can autonomously curate training data, significantly enhancing model performance across various domains.

 - Large Language Models are being evaluated for their ability to automate data engineering tasks.
 - Experiments demonstrate that LLMs can vastly improve model specialization through autonomous data adaptations.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30407

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have led to the exploration of autonomous agent-driven data engineering, which aims to enhance model specialization through effective data curation. Experiments indicate that using autonomous LLMs for data engineering can significantly improve training outcomes, marking a potential shift in how models adapt to specialized domains.

 - Autonomous data engineering evaluates LLMs to enhance model specialization.
 - Experiments showed substantial improvements in model performance with agent-driven data adaptation.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30407

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Research advancements are being made in the understanding of co-speech gestures, specifically how to bridge motion and meaning. The proposed method improves the retrieval and generation of gestures by grounding them in natural language, enhancing the communicative intent behind gestures.

 - The research focuses on improving gesture retrieval and synthesis in relation to spoken text.
 - New methods are proposed that enhance the understanding of gestures by providing structured, semantic context.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30608

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in the understanding and retrieval of co-speech gestures have been made through a method called semantic motion anchors, which establishes a connection between spoken text and gestures. This approach enhances the ability to retrieve meaningful gestures aligned with communicative intent, rather than relying solely on motion patterns. The study indicates improved retrieval metrics for gestures, showcasing a preference for semantically grounded gestures in communication tasks.

 - The proposed method improves semantics in gesture retrieval by using structured descriptions.
 - Users preferred gestures generated from semantically grounded retrieval over generic motions.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30608

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent research has unified various compression methods for large language models (LLMs), revealing that while theoretical improvements exist, practical applications suffer significant degradation in performance metrics. The authors conclude that traditional weight reconstruction objectives are insufficient, suggesting a shift towards optimizing per-layer activation rather than cross-layer methods to enhance model efficiency.

 - Recent advances in LLM compression methods demonstrate theoretical improvements but practical failures.
 - The study suggests future compression strategies should focus on per-layer activation optimization.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30836

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in large language model (LLM) compression reveal that traditional methods may not be effective in practical applications. A new approach demonstrated improved weight reconstruction errors theoretically but resulted in decreased performance on important metrics such as perplexity and accuracy. Consequently, future research is suggested to focus more on per-layer activation reconstruction instead of cross-layer optimization for better outcomes.

 - Unified optimization problem for LLM compression using SVD methods.
 - Theoretical improvements in weight reconstruction may not translate to practical application.
 - Focus shifts towards per-layer activation reconstruction for future model improvements.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30836

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Research in multi-omics data classification seeks to improve disease diagnosis efficiency while minimizing costs associated with data acquisition. A new reinforcement learning framework, SDM-Q, allows for adaptive decision-making to reduce unnecessary data collection while maintaining clinical performance. This innovation is relevant for enhancing workflows in precision medicine.

 - Multi-omics data enhances molecular characterization of diseases.
 - SDM-Q optimizes the classification process by balancing accuracy with acquisition costs.
 - The framework demonstrates that fewer omics modalities can still achieve high classification accuracy.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31014

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new reinforcement learning framework called SDM-Q has been developed for adaptive and cost-aware classification of multi-omics data in precision medicine. This approach aims to optimize the acquisition of necessary molecular modalities for disease diagnosis, reducing redundancy while maintaining classification performance. Experiments show that a single omics modality is sufficient for accurate classification in many cases, improving the efficiency of clinical workflows.

 - SDM-Q efficiently balances diagnostic utility and acquisition cost in multi-omics classification.
 - The framework allows for competitive classification performance with fewer acquired modalities.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31014

Research has introduced a framework called Light Interaction designed to enhance interactive video world models by accelerating inference without the need for retraining. This method achieves significant speed improvements in generating video content in response to user movements, making it advantageous for applications like game simulation and virtual environment navigation.

 - Light Interaction is an acceleration framework for interactive video generation.
 - It achieves up to 2.59x speedup without requiring model retraining.

Importance: 5
A new framework called Light Interaction has been developed for interactive video world models, allowing for significant speed improvements in real-time video simulations without the need for retraining the underlying model. This method enhances the efficiency of processing user interactions by utilizing adaptive computation and memory management techniques, achieving up to 2.59 times faster inference. The advancements could benefit applications in gaming, virtual reality, and artificial intelligence training.

 - Light Interaction enables faster inference in interactive video simulations.
 - The framework maintains visual quality while accelerating processing times.

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in computational chemistry involve a new method called Scalable Inference-Time Annealing (SITA), which enhances the efficiency of sampling the Boltzmann distribution of molecules. This technique utilizes flow-based models along a temperature ladder to generate samples more quickly while reducing computation costs associated with traditional methods.

 - SITA improves the sampling of molecular distributions by efficiently retraining flow-based models.
 - The method demonstrates significant performance improvements on specific molecular models while minimizing computational costs.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31498

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in generative modeling have proposed new methods for efficiently sampling the Boltzmann distribution of molecules, addressing challenges in computational chemistry and biophysics. The introduced technique, scalable inference-time annealing (SITA), utilizes flow-based models to generate samples at lower temperatures without incurring heavy computational costs associated with divergence terms.

 - Introduction of scalable inference-time annealing (SITA) for efficient sampling in molecular modeling.
 - SITA avoids costly divergence terms while providing state-of-the-art performance for certain peptides.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31498

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Research in distributed computing has led to the development of a framework for conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs), which allows for concurrent updates by multiple users without requiring global coordination. This framework uses Datalog to specify and analyze the semantics of CRDTs and their implementations, enabling more efficient collaborative applications. A case study demonstrates its effectiveness in validating correctness and scalability through property-based testing.

 - The research introduces a declarative framework for CRDTs, enhancing collaboration in distributed applications.
 - The study employs property-based testing to assess the performance and correctness of CRDT implementations.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31569

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new framework for Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs) has been developed to support local-first collaboration in distributed applications, allowing multiple users to update shared data concurrently without requiring global coordination. This framework leverages Datalog for specifying and analyzing the semantics of CRDT interactions, offering a systematic approach for correctness validation and scalability in collaborative editing contexts.

 - A Datalog framework has been introduced for analyzing and prototyping CRDTs.
 - The framework allows for concurrent updates in distributed applications without external coordination.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31569

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new mechanism called Attention Run-time Termination (ART) has been developed to improve the efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs) during long-context decoding. This approach allows for up to a 20% increase in generation throughput by dynamically terminating unnecessary accesses to Key-Value caches without compromising output quality.

 - ART is a lightweight mechanism that maximizes efficiency in long-context decoding for LLMs.
 - The method effectively reduces processing time and resource use while maintaining output quality.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00024

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new mechanism called Attention Run-time Termination (ART) has been developed for large language models to enhance the efficiency of decoding processes. The method improves generation throughput by up to 20% while maintaining the quality of the output by dynamically terminating unnecessary key-value accesses during execution.

 - ART optimizes long-context decoding in large language models.
 - The mechanism increases throughput without sacrificing output quality.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00024

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A novel framework for image generation called MIND has been developed, which incorporates explicit modeling of data manifold geometry. This approach improves image quality metrics significantly when compared to previous models. The code for this framework will be made publicly available for further research.

 - MIND integrates discrete patch tokenization into a continuous diffusion model for better image generation.
 - The framework significantly reduces the Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) compared to existing image generation models.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00094

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A novel framework for image generation called MIND has been developed, which incorporates explicit modeling of data manifold geometry. This approach improves image quality metrics significantly when compared to previous models. The code for this framework will be made publicly available for further research.

 - MIND integrates discrete patch tokenization into a continuous diffusion model for better image generation.
 - The framework significantly reduces the Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) compared to existing image generation models.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00094

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new model called Continuous Reasoning for Vision-Language-Action has been proposed, which enhances the performance of robots by refining how they link natural language, vision, and action through structured continuous thinking. This approach improves action prediction and control in robotic systems by fostering a shareable and verifiable reasoning medium. Empirical results indicate significant advancements in task success rates on real robotic platforms.

 - Continuous Reasoning enhances the integration of language and action in robotic systems.
 - The model facilitates better action prediction and control through a shareable reasoning medium.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00229

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) modeling focus on creating a continuous reasoning framework that enhances action prediction accuracy in robotic systems. This model proposes a shared, verifiable internal reasoning language that improves performance metrics on real robots significantly, suggesting new directions for AI-driven robotics. The findings indicate that operational efficiency in robotics may rely less on token-based processes and more on robust internal structures for reasoning and action coordination.

 - The continuous reasoning model improves action prediction in VLA systems for robotics.
 - It emphasizes the need for a shared and verifiable reasoning medium across model instances.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00229

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new framework called VESTA uses visual exploration with statistical tool agents to enhance the automation of fitting quantitative models to data in scientific workflows. This system improves on existing methods by dynamically creating diagnostic tools that assist in data modeling, particularly for complex tasks in fields like astronomy.

 - VESTA equips visual language models with a toolkit for data transformation and hypothesis visualization.
 - The system outperforms previous models by dynamically generating sophisticated diagnostic tools based on specific tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00384

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new framework called VESTA, which utilizes language and vision-language models, has been developed to enhance the automation of fitting quantitative models to data in scientific workflows. This system allows for dynamic tool creation that improves model refinement and performs better on complex tasks compared to existing systems. A benchmark dataset, DAWN, has also been introduced to evaluate the framework's performance on various modeling challenges in real-world applications like astronomy.

 - VESTA enhances quantitative modeling automation in scientific workflows.
 - The framework shows improved performance on complex tasks by dynamically creating diagnostic tools.
 - DAWN is a new benchmark dataset designed for evaluating model performance in various scientific contexts.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00384

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new predictive learning model called CR-JEPA has been developed to enhance remote sensing image retrieval across different modalities. The model addresses difficulties in retrieving semantically related scenes by improving both cross-modal and same-modal retrieval processes, achieving significant accuracy improvements on benchmark datasets.

 - CR-JEPA model improves cross-modal semantic alignment in remote sensing.
 - It utilizes modality-specific structures and shared transformer architecture.
 - The model outperforms previous benchmarks in retrieval accuracy.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00706

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new model called CR-JEPA has been developed for cross-modal remote sensing image retrieval, addressing challenges associated with retrieving semantically related scenes across different sensing modalities. The model improves retrieval performance significantly on benchmark datasets while employing innovative techniques to enhance retrieval stability and efficiency.

 - CR-JEPA is designed to overcome the challenges in cross-modal remote sensing image retrieval, which have been difficult due to variations in imaging physics and visual appearance.
 - The model utilizes a unique architecture that integrates modality-specific components and a shared transformer framework to optimize both same-modal and cross-modal retrieval.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00706

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in video-based world models show a gap in maintaining stable internal states over time, despite improvements in synthesizing visual sequences. A comprehensive benchmark called MBench has been introduced to evaluate the memory capabilities of these models across three dimensions: entity consistency, environment consistency, and causal consistency. Evaluations reveal significant limitations in long-term state retention of existing methods, providing direction for future research.

 - MBench assesses memory capability in video world models.
 - It identifies critical gaps in long-term state retention of existing models.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00793

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in video-based world models have shown the ability to synthesize high-fidelity visual sequences, yet challenges remain in maintaining a consistent internal state over long temporal horizons. A new benchmark called MBench has been introduced to assess the memory capabilities of these models across entity consistency, environment consistency, and causal consistency, aiming to advance research in this field.

 - MBench provides a systematic approach to evaluate memory capability in video world models.
 - It identifies critical limitations in existing methods for long-term state retention.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00793

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Research focuses on a new framework called Individual Fairness-aware Strategic Classification (IFSC), which addresses the interdependent manipulations in scenarios where fairness is based on individual outcomes rather than group outcomes. This framework models agents' behavior as they imitate peers in order to achieve favorable results, incorporating elements of uncertainty in their observations.

 - The framework IFSC aims to improve fairness in predictive models by addressing how agents manipulate their attributes based on peer outcomes.
 - Experiments show that IFSC enhances consistency in individual fairness and reduces distortions caused by imitation.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00827

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Research is advancing in the field of strategic classification, focusing on individual fairness and how agents manipulate their characteristics to influence outcomes. A new framework has been introduced that models these manipulations as peer imitation, allowing for better alignment of decision-making processes with fairness criteria. Experiments indicate that this new approach improves consistency in fairness and reduces distortions caused by peer influence.

 - Strategic classification examines how individuals manipulate their features for favorable predictive outcomes.
 - The individual fairness-aware strategic classification framework recognizes interdependent manipulations based on peer influences.
 - Experimental results show improved fairness consistency and reduced distortions in outcomes.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00827

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new multimodal framework for generating high-resolution 3D CT images has been developed, which allows flexibility in using both text descriptions and segmentation prompts without the need for full-organ annotations. This approach improves data efficiency and enhances the controllability of medical image generation, achieving state-of-the-art results in perceptual and semantic assessments.

 - A generative model for volumetric medical images focuses on enhancing controllability through a flexible framework.
 - The method integrates text prompts and segmentation masks to generate anatomically consistent CT volumes with improved data efficiency.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00967

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new multimodal framework for generating high-resolution 3D CT images offers greater control over image generation, allowing for input from both text prompts and partial segmentation masks. This model is designed to improve data efficiency and accuracy in medical imaging, showcasing state-of-the-art performance metrics compared to existing methods.

 - The MedSyn2 framework allows flexible control in generating 3D CT images using text and semantic segmentation.
 - Experiments show significant improvements in image quality and data efficiency for medical imaging applications.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00967

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Research in language models indicates that alignment tuning affects the internal computations of these systems, leading to selective changes based on the type of concepts processed. Studies show that normative concepts exhibit greater shifts compared to factual ones, and these changes are localized within specific layers of the model architecture, suggesting that alignment has measurable effects beyond observable behavior.

 - Alignment tuning in language models causes selective internal changes in computation.
 - Normative concepts show greater shifts than factual ones during alignment.
 - Measurable changes in internal layer structures suggest alignment impacts extend beyond surface-level behavior.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01060

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent research presented a framework called MENTIS that investigates how preference alignment affects the internal mechanics of language models. The study found that alignment leads to selective changes in model behavior and internal computational structures, with different effects depending on the types of concepts involved. Results suggest that post-alignment modifications are not uniform and can vary significantly across different model architectures.

 - MENTIS framework measures internal changes in language models due to preference alignment.
 - Alignment-induced changes are selective, affecting normative concepts more than factual ones.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01060

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new framework called ImagineUAV has been proposed for aerial navigation using a vision-language model. This innovative approach combines world-action modeling with kinodynamic planning, improving the flight execution and environmental interaction of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). Preliminary results indicate that ImagineUAV significantly outperforms existing models in both simulation and practical applications.

 - ImagineUAV utilizes an advanced framework for UAV navigation through vision and language interpretation.
 - The system has demonstrated superior performance in generating collision-free flight paths compared to previous models.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01205

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in UAV technology involve a framework called ImagineUAV that enhances aerial navigation using vision-language models, which integrate dynamic modeling and planning. This system overcomes previous limitations by applying imagination-driven techniques to generate real-time, collision-free flight trajectories, demonstrating improved performance in practical applications.

 - ImagineUAV utilizes a novel approach to improve drone navigation using language-based commands.
 - The framework surpasses existing models in both simulated benchmarks and real-world scenarios.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01205

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Research in machine learning focuses on improving hard negative mining techniques for retrieval systems, addressing current limitations associated with negative sample selection and contamination by false positives. A new approach, termed CausalNeg, aims to bridge the gap between generative and discriminative models to enhance the effectiveness of these techniques in training retrieval systems.

 - Hard negative mining is essential for training effective retrieval systems but faces limitations.
 - CausalNeg offers a novel method to improve the generation of negative samples for better performance in retrieval tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01304

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in hard negative mining techniques for training information retrieval systems have revealed limitations due to false positives and reliance on corpus availability. Researchers have introduced a new method called CausalNeg, which uses controlled data construction and training techniques to enhance the performance and reliability of these systems.

 - Hard negative mining is essential for training effective retrievers but has intrinsic limitations.
 - CausalNeg represents a new approach to improve the contrastive learning process by addressing the generative-discriminative gap.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01304

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Research in learning-augmented paging focuses on improving robustness in algorithm performance, particularly regarding predictions. Recent findings propose a new framework that optimally closes the gap in competitive ratios for such systems, demonstrating significant practical advantages in various applications.

 - Learning-augmented paging algorithms have been shown to be beneficial for real-world applications due to their bounded robustness.
 - A new framework has been developed that achieves the best possible robustness in learning-augmented paging, enhancing algorithm competitiveness.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01342

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in learning-augmented paging are addressing issues of robustness in machine learning applications, specifically focusing on achieving optimal performance even with inaccurate predictions. A new framework has been developed that significantly closes the gap in robustness bounds for these algorithms, enhancing their practical applicability in real-world systems.

 - Learning-augmented paging algorithms provide bounded robustness, ensuring performance despite inaccuracies.
 - Research introduces a new framework that optimizes robustness, achieving performance improvements within existing theoretical limits.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01342

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in parameter-efficient continual learning have emerged, focusing on methods that allow for backward knowledge transfer without the typical issue of catastrophic forgetting. A proposed framework, named Selective Backward Refinement, enables controlled backward transfer while maintaining performance across multiple benchmarks.

 - The Selective Backward Refinement (SABER) framework addresses limitations in existing continuous learning methodologies.
 - Extensive experiments demonstrate that SABER can improve past task performance while preserving overall effectiveness.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01379

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in parameter-efficient continual learning have been made through a new framework known as Selective Backward Refinement for positive Backward knowledge transfER (SABER). This framework allows controlled backward transfer, addressing limitations in traditional prompt-based methods by determining when backward refinement is advantageous and performing it without interfering with prior tasks.

 - The SABER framework improves backward knowledge transfer in continual learning.
 - SABER ensures updates are safe by restricting them to non-interfering directions.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01379

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new drone simulation platform named Crazyflow has been developed, aiming to enhance aerial robotics by providing a faster and more accurate means for training and developing algorithms. This simulator allows for high-fidelity simulations, supporting both individual and swarm drone operations, and enables real-time learning and optimization during flight.

 - Crazyflow achieves significantly faster simulation speeds compared to existing drone simulators.
 - The platform allows for in-flight reinforcement learning and real-time algorithm development.
 - It supports multiple simulation abstractions and is compatible with open-source Crazyflie models.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01478

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
A new drone simulator named Crazyflow has been developed, which allows for high-speed, accurate simulations of aerial robotics. It supports large-scale synthetic data generation for drone algorithms, enabling rapid reconfiguration and in-flight learning capabilities, significantly advancing the field of robotics and artificial intelligence.

 - Crazyflow achieves speeds surpassing existing drone simulators, enabling the simulation of thousands of drones simultaneously.
 - The simulator allows for unprecedented in-flight reinforcement learning and adaptability across various drone applications.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01478

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in machine learning focus on developing flexible algorithms for online representation learning, particularly in high-dimensional spaces. These new methods are designed to handle sparse representations effectively, enabling better results in community detection, clustering, and feature learning applications across large data sets.

 - A new algorithm is proposed for online biologically plausible learning that enhances data representation.
 - The algorithm addresses challenges of conventional methods that struggle with high-dimensional data and scalability.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01546

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new online learning algorithm has been developed for sparse high-dimensional representations, which are important for various data analysis methods like clustering and feature learning. This algorithm can handle large datasets and is designed to optimize computations that have previously been impractical due to complexity.

 - The algorithm learns sparse shift-invariant representations for data analysis.
 - It addresses computational issues in existing methods by being applicable to large data sets.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01546

Category: Cybersecurity
Importance: 8
Large language model (LLM) agents are being increasingly targeted by a new type of attack known as skill injection attacks, which exploit the dependency of these agents on reusable task-specific skills. Research shows that implementing guardian-based defenses can significantly reduce the success rate of these attacks, demonstrating that real-time mediation of skill file access is an effective protective measure against such threats.

 - Skill injection attacks target LLM agents using reusable task-specific documentation.
 - Guardian-based defenses can reduce the attack success rate significantly.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01567

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in reinforcement learning (RL) frameworks focus on improving the creation and optimization of skills utilized by AI agents, enabling them to develop more effective strategies that can generalize across various tasks. The introduction of a new method, ReSkill, allows for continuous refinement of skills alongside policy learning, demonstrating improved performance in unseen tasks. This evolution in AI capabilities has implications for various applications in artificial intelligence and machine learning.

 - ReSkill is a new framework that integrates skill creation with policy optimization in reinforcement learning.
 - The method improves the efficiency of AI agents by allowing them to create and refine skills dynamically as they learn.
 - ReSkill has shown better performance in tasks not previously encountered, indicating its potential for broader applications.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01619

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in agentic reinforcement learning (RL) focus on integrating skill creation with policy optimization to enhance performance across various tasks. The new framework, ReSkill, improves upon existing methods by automatically creating and refining reusable skills while aligning them with ongoing policy learning, leading to significant gains in task adaptability.

 - ReSkill reconciles skill creation and policy optimization in agentic RL.
 - The framework demonstrates improved learning across unseen tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01619

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Large language models (LLMs) are being used in multi-agent systems, where they respond to answers given by other models. Research shows that these LLMs often conform to majority opinions, which can lead to the propagation of errors rather than corrections, particularly when responding to peer influences and authority cues.

 - LLMs can be misled by peer consensus, making it hard for them to correct their own mistakes.
 - Authority cues influence LLMs to prefer endorsed answers, irrespective of their correctness.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01637

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Large language models (LLMs) used in multi-agent systems face challenges related to conformity, where they may adopt majority answers that could be incorrect. New research indicates that peer consensus can mislead models more easily than it can correct them, raising the question of whether LLMs should verify peer responses instead of simply aggregating them.

 - Large language models can be influenced by peer agreement, leading to errors.
 - Authority labels can cause LLMs to follow endorsed answers regardless of their correctness.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01637

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly utilized in public discourse, leading to a phenomenon known as 'argument collapse,' where diverse human arguments are replaced by a limited range of LLM-generated responses. Research indicates that while human debates contain a significant number of unique arguments, LLM outputs often show a stark reduction in variability and originality. This trend is prominent in both short-form and long-form discussions, suggesting a potential impact on the quality of public debate.

 - LLMs are impacting the diversity of arguments in public debates.
 - Human-generated arguments are largely unique compared to those produced by LLMs.
 - The issue of argument collapse occurs in various formats of debate, including long-form essays.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01736

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
The increasing use of large language models (LLMs) in drafting public debates is resulting in 'argument collapse', where generated arguments converge to a smaller set of main points, diminishing diversity in public discourse. A study indicates that while humans present unique arguments in debates, LLM-generated responses are often repetitive and lack the depth of human reasoning.

 - LLMs generate a limited variety of arguments compared to human writers.
 - The structure of LLM essays often follows a fixed pattern, unlike the more varied approaches of human authors.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01736

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in language model serving technology focus on optimizing cache mechanisms to improve efficiency. A new method called SparseX allows for segment-level caching, enhancing performance during multi-turn interactions and complex tasks. This approach is designed to work across various applications without requiring additional training or preprocessing.

 - SparseX is a segment-level KV cache sharing method aimed at improving long-context language model serving.
 - It enables better reuse of token segments, enhancing performance in applications like multi-round chat and retrieval-augmented generation.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01751

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new method called SparseX has been introduced to improve the efficiency of segment-level key-value cache sharing for processing long-context language models. This technique aims to address issues with conventional caching mechanisms used in AI by enabling better reuse of content across multiple requests and contexts, thus optimizing computational resources and enhancing performance.

 - SparseX enhances existing caching methods for long-context language models.
 - The method allows more efficient reuse of token segments in complex interleaved tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01751

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
The development of WorldCoder-Bench is advancing the capabilities of large language models to create interactive 3D environments based on natural language input. A new benchmarking framework has been introduced that assesses the accuracy and efficiency of these models in synthesizing physically grounded 3D worlds, with substantial implications for the fields of artificial intelligence and web generation.

 - WorldCoder-Bench includes 2,026 tasks for evaluating 3D world synthesis in various scenarios.
 - The best performing models achieve limited verification coverage, with challenges related to state management and user interaction.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01869

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Research is advancing in the field of 3D world synthesis, utilizing large language models (LLMs) to construct interactive environments from natural language inputs. A new benchmark, WorldCoder-Bench, has been introduced to evaluate these systems, focusing on their ability to create and maintain simulations that adhere to physical and spatial constraints.

 - WorldCoder-Bench includes 2,026 expert-curated tasks for evaluating 3D world synthesis.
 - The benchmark highlights the limitations of current LLMs in achieving high verification coverage for generated environments.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01869

Category: Technology
Importance: 8
Recent advancements in robot manipulation technology involve integrating 3D spatial awareness with end-to-end learning models. Researchers are addressing limitations of 2D inputs to enhance robots' adaptability and effectiveness in real-world tasks through novel data processing and alignment strategies.

 - New methods are proposed for aligning 3D representations with robotic policies.
 - This research aims to improve the generalization of robotic manipulation through advanced data pipelines.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02274

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in robotics have been made through the development of a model called Dexterity-BEV, which seeks to enhance the manipulation capabilities of robots by aligning 2D visual inputs with their underlying 3D nature. This methodology addresses significant limitations of current models by improving spatial awareness and error reduction in real-world robotic actions, enabling better generalization across diverse robot configurations and settings.

 - Dexterity-BEV proposes a pixel-wise 3D representation to improve robotic manipulation by aligning 2D inputs with 3D environments.
 - The model includes a comprehensive data processing pipeline and a temporal alignment scheme to facilitate consistent training and evaluation across various robots and datasets.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02274

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new dual-encoder architecture has been developed for underwater acoustic classification, addressing challenges present in complex acoustic environments. This method combines waveform and spectrogram inputs using a differentiable fuzzy aggregation mechanism to improve classification accuracy while minimizing the risk of overfitting amidst limited data and computational costs.

 - The architecture processes both acoustic waveforms and spectrograms for improved classification.
 - Introduces a novel gating mechanism that helps mitigate challenges due to non-stationary underwater environments.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02341

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Recent advancements in underwater acoustic classification have led to the development of a dual-encoder neural architecture that efficiently processes both waveforms and spectrograms. This new approach addresses challenges in the underwater environment and improves classification accuracy while controlling computational costs and the risk of overfitting on limited datasets.

 - A dual-encoder neural architecture has been proposed to enhance underwater acoustic classification.
 - The approach employs a novel differentiable aggregation mechanism to improve accuracy and interpretability.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02341

Category: Technology
Importance: 6
Recent advancements in Bayesian optimization are being made, particularly through the development of local Preferential Bayesian Optimization (PBO) methods that enhance efficiency in complex optimization problems. These methods leverage pairwise human feedback to improve search in high-dimensional landscapes, which could greatly benefit real-world applications such as policy search.

 - Local PBO methods adapt trust-region and derivative-informed local search techniques for optimizing preferences.
 - The new approach has shown significant improvements in reducing cumulative regret in real-world optimization tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02351

---


Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in machine learning have led to the development of local preferential Bayesian optimization methods. These methods improve the efficiency of optimization tasks by adapting techniques from high-dimensional Bayesian optimization, particularly for complex landscapes with steep optima.

 - Local Bayesian optimization can effectively tune expensive, noisy experiments without needing an explicit objective function.
 - The introduction of local PBO methods allows for better handling of high-dimensional and complex optimization tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02351

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A new game-theoretic decision framework has been developed for selecting coordination detection methods in multi-UAV fleet operations, addressing the trade-off between speed and accuracy in real-time traffic management. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the framework provides distinct method portfolios based on operational priorities, enhancing the performance of UAV fleet monitoring.

 - The framework formulates method selection as a two-player zero-sum game to optimize coordination detection.
 - It identifies optimal portfolios of detection methods based on operational priorities, enhancing fleet behavior monitoring.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02383

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
A game-theoretic framework has been developed to optimize coordination detection methods for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) fleets in shared airspace, addressing the trade-off between speed and accuracy in traffic management. By utilizing a two-player zero-sum game approach, this research identifies robust computational strategies for monitoring UAV operations across various traffic scenarios.

 - A decision framework is introduced to choose detection methods for UAV fleet coordination.
 - The study demonstrates a trade-off between speed and accuracy in real-time traffic management.
 - Experimental evaluations highlight distinct method portfolios based on operational priorities.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02383

Category: Technology

Agricultural robots are increasingly being integrated into farming tasks, but traditional models assume that the instructions they receive are always correct, which is often not the case. Researchers have developed a new benchmark and an instructional analysis module to improve the robots' ability to detect and correct mistakes in navigation instructions, resulting in notable performance enhancements.

 - Agricultural robots currently rely on potentially incorrect natural language instructions for navigation.
 - A new benchmark and analysis module have been created to help agricultural robots identify and rectify errors in their instructions.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02519

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
Agricultural robots are increasingly integral to farming, yet they often struggle with instructions that contain errors. Recent research has introduced a benchmark and a module to improve robots' ability to recognize and correct these mistakes, enhancing their operational efficiency.

 - A new method has been developed to help these robots recognize and address instruction mistakes, improving their performance.

Category: Technology

A new framework called S2 (See Less, Specify More) has been developed to enhance the generalization abilities of vision-language-action (VLA) models. This framework aims to improve performance in real-robot tasks by training executors to rely on task-specific visual evidence while minimizing distractions from irrelevant visual contexts.

 - S2 framework improves VLA generalization by training executors to act from informative local guidance.
 - The framework demonstrates improved task success rates in various real-robot applications.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02735

Category: Technology
Importance: 7
A new framework has been developed to enhance the generalization of vision-language-action models by streamlining the execution process. The approach emphasizes the use of targeted visual evidence, resulting in improved task performance metrics in robotic applications.

 - The S2 framework improves VLA generalization by training executors with a cleaner interface.
 - This method leads to significant improvements in subtask success rates in real-robot tasks.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02735

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent research in deep learning architecture indicates that using context-free value vectors in transformer models may enhance performance. A new method called Bank of Values (BoV) proposes storing these value vectors more efficiently, thereby improving model accuracy with reduced computational requirements.

 - Transformers may perform better with context-free value vectors.
 - Bank of Values (BoV) offers a new approach to computing attention value vectors.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02780

Category: Technology
Importance: 5
Recent advancements in deep learning architecture, particularly in transformers, show improved performance when using context-free value vectors in attention mechanisms. Researchers propose a new method called Bank of Values (BoV), which enables models to learn token-specific value vectors efficiently, leading to better validation loss and benchmark scores with reduced computational requirements.

 - The effectiveness of context-free value vectors in transformer models enhances performance.
 - The Bank of Values method optimizes the computation and memory involved in managing value vectors.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02780

The model achieves state-of-the-art performance in several tasks and aims to facilitate advancements in the field of Physical AI by providing open access to its code and datasets.

 - Cosmos 3 combines language, image, video, audio, and action processing into a single framework.
 - The model sets new benchmarks in performance for various AI tasks and is made available for open research.

Category: Technology
Importance: 8

 - Cosmos 3 is an omnimodal world model capable of processing language, images, videos, audio, and actions in a unified architecture.

A new multimodal reasoning framework called ChatHealthAI has been developed to improve clinical decision support by aligning electronic health record representations with large language models.  - ChatHealthAI integrates structured electronic health record representations with large language models.

A new multimodal reasoning framework called ChatHealthAI has been developed to enhance the integration of electronic health records (EHR) with large language models (LLMs) for clinical decision support. This system aims to improve the interpretability and reasoning capabilities of LLMs while maintaining their predictive performance, particularly in medical contexts.

 - ChatHealthAI aligns EHR representations with LLMs for improved clinical reasoning.

Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02802
